title: Public Identity, Privacy, Security, and Reputation Boundaries
description: A learner is told to make everything public, revealing contact details, location patterns, secrets, client material, protected identity, private history, and unsafe availability. Learners cumulatively produce public-identity threat and disclosure model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.
Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
Model impersonation, harassment, discrimination, credential theft, doxxing, scraping, and relationship harm for the learner context.
